questions.question: Water exists as solid ice, liquid water and steam. In which states can water easily be compressed to half its original volume? A ice, liquid water and steam, B liquid water and steam only, C liquid water only, D steam only
questions.text: Water exists as solid ice, liquid water and steam. In which states can water easily be compressed to half its original volume? A ice, liquid water and steam, B liquid water and steam only, C liquid water only, D steam only
options: A ice, liquid water and steam
B liquid water and steam only
C liquid water only
D steam only

questions.question: The melting point of sulfur is 113 °C. Which row describes the arrangement and movement of sulfur particles at 25 °C? arrangement of particles movement of particles A regular vibrate about fixed positions, B regular random, C random vibrate about fixed positions, D random random
questions.text: The melting point of sulfur is 113 °C. Which row describes the arrangement and movement of sulfur particles at 25 °C? arrangement of particles movement of particles A regular vibrate about fixed positions, B regular random, C random vibrate about fixed positions, D random random
options: A regular vibrate about fixed positions
B regular random
C random vibrate about fixed positions
D random random

questions.question: Solid Y is added to an excess of hot water. A blue solution forms and a brown solid remains. The brown solid is filtered off and dried. The brown solid conducts electricity. What is Y?, A a compound that contains a metal, B a mixture that contains a metal, C a pure substance that is a metal, D a pure substance that is a non-metal
questions.text: Solid Y is added to an excess of hot water. A blue solution forms and a brown solid remains. The brown solid is filtered off and dried. The brown solid conducts electricity. What is Y?, A a compound that contains a metal, B a mixture that contains a metal, C a pure substance that is a metal, D a pure substance that is a non-metal
options: A blue solution forms and a brown solid remains. The brown solid is filtered off and dried. The brown solid conducts electricity. What is Y?
A a compound that contains a metal
B a mixture that contains a metal
C a pure substance that is a metal
D a pure substance that is a non-metal

questions.question: Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct? A Electrons and protons each have a relative mass of 1, and neutrons have no charge., B Electrons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge., C Protons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge., D Neutrons have a relative mass of 1 and no charge. 3
questions.text: Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct? A Electrons and protons each have a relative mass of 1, and neutrons have no charge., B Electrons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge., C Protons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge., D Neutrons have a relative mass of 1 and no charge. 3
options: A Electrons and protons each have a relative mass of 1, and neutrons have no charge.
B Electrons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge.
C Protons have a relative mass of 1 and a negative charge.
D Neutrons have a relative mass of 1 and no charge. 3

B isotopes
C mass number
D the group number

questions.question: The electronic configurations of atoms Q and R are shown. Q R Q and R form an ionic compound. What is the formula of the compound? A R 7 Q B R 4 Q 2, C RQ D RQ 7 4
questions.text: The electronic configurations of atoms Q and R are shown. Q R Q and R form an ionic compound. What is the formula of the compound? A R 7 Q B R 4 Q 2, C RQ D RQ 7 4
options: A R 7 Q B R 4 Q 2
C RQ D RQ 7 4

questions.question: Which properties are typical of simple molecules? 1   high melting point 2   good electrical conductivity 3   low boiling point 4   high malleability A 1 and 2 B  2 and 4 C  3 and 4 D  3 only
questions.text: Which properties are typical of simple molecules? 1   high melting point 2   good electrical conductivity 3   low boiling point 4   high malleability A 1 and 2 B  2 and 4 C  3 and 4 D  3 only
options: A 1 and 2 B  2 and 4 C  3 and 4 D  3 only

questions.question: Diamond is extremely hard and does not conduct electricity. Which statement explains these properties? A It has positive carbon ions surrounded by delocalised electrons., B It has strong ionic bonds between each carbon atom., C It has delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms three covalent bonds with other carbon atoms., D It has no delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms four covalent bonds with other carbon atoms.
questions.text: Diamond is extremely hard and does not conduct electricity. Which statement explains these properties? A It has positive carbon ions surrounded by delocalised electrons., B It has strong ionic bonds between each carbon atom., C It has delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms three covalent bonds with other carbon atoms., D It has no delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms four covalent bonds with other carbon atoms.
options: A It has positive carbon ions surrounded by delocalised electrons.
B It has strong ionic bonds between each carbon atom.
C It has delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms three covalent bonds with other carbon atoms.
D It has no delocalised electrons, and each carbon atom forms four covalent bonds with other carbon atoms.

questions.question: Which equation represents the complete combustion of butane, C 4 H 10 ? A 2C 4 H 10 +  5O 2   8C  +  10H 2 O, B 2C 4 H 10 +  9O 2   8CO  +  10H 2 O, C 2C 4 H 10 +  13O 2   8CO 2 +  10H 2 O, D C 4 H 10 +  4O 2   4CO 2 +  5H 2

questions.question: When pink cobalt(II) chloride is heated, it forms a blue solid and water. When water is added to the blue solid, it releases heat and a pink solid is formed. Which row describes the type of reaction that occurs and the pink cobalt( II) chloride? type of reaction pink cobalt( II) chloride A non-reversible anhydrous, B non-reversible hydrated, C reversible anhydrous, D reversible hydrated
questions.text: When pink cobalt(II) chloride is heated, it forms a blue solid and water. When water is added to the blue solid, it releases heat and a pink solid is formed. Which row describes the type of reaction that occurs and the pink cobalt( II) chloride? type of reaction pink cobalt( II) chloride A non-reversible anhydrous, B non-reversible hydrated, C reversible anhydrous, D reversible hydrated
options: A non-reversible anhydrous
B non-reversible hydrated
C reversible anhydrous
D reversible hydrated

questions.question: Which substances react with dilute hydrochloric acid to form aqueous copper( II) chloride? 1   copper 2   copper( II) oxide 3   copper( II) carbonate 4   copper( II) hydroxide A 1, 2, 3 and 4, B 1, 2 and 3 only, C 2, 3 and 4 only, D 2 and 3 only 7
questions.text: Which substances react with dilute hydrochloric acid to form aqueous copper( II) chloride? 1   copper 2   copper( II) oxide 3   copper( II) carbonate 4   copper( II) hydroxide A 1, 2, 3 and 4, B 1, 2 and 3 only, C 2, 3 and 4 only, D 2 and 3 only 7
options: A 1, 2, 3 and 4
B 1, 2 and 3 only
C 2, 3 and 4 only
D 2 and 3 only 7

questions.question: A  few  drops  of  thymolphthalein  and  methyl  orange  are  added  separately  to  two  test-tubes containing solution X. Solution Y is then added to both test-tubes until present in excess. The colours of the indicators before and after addition of solution Y are shown. colour of thymolphthalein colour of methyl orange solution X only colourless red mixture of solution X and excess solution Y blue yellow What is solution Y? A HCl (aq) B NaOH(aq) C H 2 O(l) D NaCl (aq)

questions.question: Iron is extracted from hematite in a blast furnace. Which reaction involves thermal decomposition? A Calcium carbonate reacts to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide., B Carbon (coke) reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide., C Carbon dioxide reacts with carbon to form carbon monoxide., D Iron( III) oxide reacts with carbon monoxide to form iron and carbon dioxide. 10
questions.text: Iron is extracted from hematite in a blast furnace. Which reaction involves thermal decomposition? A Calcium carbonate reacts to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide., B Carbon (coke) reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide., C Carbon dioxide reacts with carbon to form carbon monoxide., D Iron( III) oxide reacts with carbon monoxide to form iron and carbon dioxide. 10
options: A Calcium carbonate reacts to form calcium oxide and carbon dioxide.
B Carbon (coke) reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide.
C Carbon dioxide reacts with carbon to form carbon monoxide.
D Iron( III) oxide reacts with carbon monoxide to form iron and carbon dioxide. 10

questions.question: Gas X is a waste gas from digestion in animals. Gas Y is formed when gas X is burned with an insufficient amount of oxygen. Gas Z is formed when gas X is burned with an excess of oxygen. What are X, Y and Z? X Y Z A carbon dioxide methane carbon monoxide, B carbon monoxide methane carbon dioxide, C methane carbon dioxide    carbon monoxide, D methane carbon monoxide    carbon dioxide
questions.text: Gas X is a waste gas from digestion in animals. Gas Y is formed when gas X is burned with an insufficient amount of oxygen. Gas Z is formed when gas X is burned with an excess of oxygen. What are X, Y and Z? X Y Z A carbon dioxide methane carbon monoxide, B carbon monoxide methane carbon dioxide, C methane carbon dioxide    carbon monoxide, D methane carbon monoxide    carbon dioxide
options: A carbon dioxide methane carbon monoxide
B carbon monoxide methane carbon dioxide
C methane carbon dioxide    carbon monoxide

B It is a reaction between carbon dioxide and water.
C It produces a gas that contributes to increased global warming.
D It requires a temperature greater than 50 °C. 11

questions.question: L and M are different homologous series. The first three members of homologous series L all decolourise aqueous bromine. The first three members of homologous series M all dissolve in water to give solutions with a pH below 7. What are the general formulae for homologous series L and M? homologous series L homologous series M A C n H 2n C n H 2n+1 COOH, B C n H 2n C n H 2n+1 OH, C C n H 2n+2 C n H 2n+1 OH, D C n H 2n+2 C n H 2n+1 COOH

B C n H 2n C n H 2n+1 OH
C C n H 2n+2 C n H 2n+1 OH
D C n H 2n+2 C n H 2n+1 COOH
